name: Request a Deferral or Leave of Absence
description: Call admissions (pre-matriculation) or the dean of students / registrar (post-matriculation) to request a deferral or leave of absence — gap year, military, mission, family, financial, mental-health, professional opportunity — and to lock down the re-entry path so the seat / financial aid / scholarships are protected.
disclaimer: Deferral (before you've started) and leave of absence (after you've matriculated) are different processes with different decision-makers and consequences. Scholarships, financial aid, and housing are sometimes preserved across leaves and sometimes not — get every promise in writing.

context.when_to_use: User has either (a) been admitted but wants to defer starting by a semester or a year, or (b) is currently enrolled and wants to step away for one or more terms without withdrawing entirely. Common reasons: gap year, financial, mental-health, family caregiving, internship / job opportunity, military deployment, religious mission, athletic Olympic training.
preconditions: User can articulate the reason and the intended return date.
User has reviewed the school's deferral / LOA policy (most schools cap deferral at 1–2 years and LOA at 1–4 terms).
User has a sense of what they'll be doing during the leave — schools are friendlier to structured plans (job, service, treatment) than to undefined time off.
User has identified anchoring deadlines: deposit deadline, financial-aid renewal, scholarship renewal, housing waitlist, health insurance, immigration status.

principles: Deferral is admissions' decision; LOA is the dean's. Calling the wrong office wastes a day.
Reasons matter for approval, but documentation matters for protection. Get the leave in writing with explicit return conditions.
Scholarships and merit aid don't automatically follow you. Ask each award separately whether it survives the leave.

Re-entry should be a checkbox, not a re-application. Negotiate a return process at the front end: 'I email by [date], you reactivate me.' If it's an interview + committee, that's a red flag for friction at return.
Federal student loans enter grace-period countdown when the student drops below half-time. A 12-month leave can exhaust the entire grace period before re-entry.
International students: an LOA usually terminates SEVIS. Plan with the DSO before filing.

phrases.opener_deferral: Hi, I've been admitted for [term] and I'd like to request a one-[semester / year] deferral. I'm planning to [activity]. Could you walk me through the process and what happens to my scholarship and aid package?
phrases.opener_loa: Hi, I'm currently enrolled and I'd like to take a leave of absence starting [term]. My intended return is [term]. Could we walk through the process — the form, the decision-maker, and what happens with aid, housing, and re-entry?
phrases.ask_about_scholarship: I have a [scholarship name] award. Does it carry over through a deferral / LOA, or do I need to re-apply, or is it forfeited? If carry-over is conditional, what are the conditions?
phrases.ask_about_aid: How does my financial-aid package handle a leave? Does Pell / institutional grant / loan eligibility reset, freeze, or expire?
phrases.ask_about_housing: What happens to my housing assignment? Am I in the next year's lottery automatically, or do I lose priority?
phrases.ask_about_re_entry: What's the re-entry process exactly? Do I email by a date, fill a form, attend an interview, or re-apply? I'd like the simplest possible path documented in writing.
phrases.ask_about_F1: I'm on F-1 status. How does an LOA affect SEVIS, and has the DSO been brought into this conversation?
phrases.ask_for_dean_of_students: Could I speak with the dean of students or associate dean? I want to make sure the LOA is set up so re-entry is straightforward.
phrases.graceful_close_success: Thank you. To confirm in writing: leave effective [date], return [date], scholarship [status], aid [status], housing [status], re-entry process [steps], and the decision letter will come from [office]. Could you email me a confirmation?

tactics.name: Route to the right office on the first call
tactics.script: If pre-matriculation: admissions. If post-matriculation: dean of students or registrar. If athlete: also compliance. If F-1/J-1: also DSO. Calling the wrong office burns a day and you'll be told to call back, often missing a deadline.

tactics.name: Pin down each financial bucket separately
tactics.script: Use `ask_about_scholarship`, `ask_about_aid`, and ask about each merit award by name. Front-line staff often answer 'yes it carries over' generically — but specific scholarships have donor-specific rules. Ask them to verify in the system.

tactics.name: Negotiate a minimal re-entry process
tactics.script: Use `ask_about_re_entry`. The ideal: 'Email by [date], the dean reactivates you, you register for classes.' Less ideal: re-application, committee review, interview. Push for the lighter option and ask for it in writing.

tactics.name: Loop the DSO before filing if F-1 / J-1
tactics.script: Use `ask_about_F1`. A leave can terminate SEVIS within 15 days unless an Authorized Early Withdrawal or Reduced Course Load is in place. The DSO is the only office that can protect status.

tactics.name: Plan a 'structured' leave narrative
tactics.script: Schools approve gap years more easily when there's a plan: internship, service, treatment, family caregiving with a defined timeline. 'I want to figure things out' is harder. Frame the time as productive even if it's recovery.

tactics.name: Escalate to the dean of students for non-standard timing
tactics.when: Mid-term LOA, multi-term LOA, or LOA across a graduation cohort.
tactics.script: Use `ask_for_dean_of_students`. Standard policies cover standard cases; non-standard cases need dean-level approval. The dean of students has discretion that registrars don't.

tactics.name: Ask about insurance + immunization holds
tactics.script: Health insurance enrollment, immunization records, and Title IX trainings often expire during a leave and create silent re-entry holds. Surface them at the LOA conversation so they don't ambush the return.

boundaries: Do NOT fabricate a reason for leave. If asked, schools may verify employers, programs, or providers.
Do NOT defer without confirming scholarship carry-over in writing — verbal yeses don't survive staff turnover.
Do NOT take an LOA without confirming the half-time-status loan implications.
Do NOT file an LOA on F-1 / J-1 without DSO involvement.
Do NOT assume housing rolls over — most schools require a new application even after approved LOA.
success_signals: Form submitted and decision date named.
Scholarship and aid carry-over confirmed in writing.
Re-entry process is one or two steps (email + form), not a re-application.
Housing path is mapped (lottery priority preserved or queue reentry).
DSO has authorized leave for F-1 students.
failure_signals: Scholarship is non-deferrable — admit-and-attend or forfeit.
Re-entry requires re-application + committee review with no protection of admit / aid.
F-1 SEVIS termination is unavoidable, requiring a new visa for return.
Mid-term LOA falls outside policy and dean's office won't grant exception.
Loans enter accelerated grace period and would come due before realistic re-entry.

exit_strategy.on_success: Confirm all of: effective date, return date, scholarship, aid, housing, insurance, immunizations, re-entry process, decision-maker office, and DSO status. Get email confirmation before the call ends.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Identify the blocker. If scholarship: explore donor petition. If F-1: explore Reduced Course Load instead of LOA. If aid grace-period: explore in-school deferment by enrolling at a community college part-time. If timing: petition the dean of students.
follow_ups: Email operator a one-pager with all confirmed dates and protections.
Calendar scholarship-renewal date during the leave year.
30 days before return, confirm health insurance, immunizations, and Title IX trainings are current.

required_user_info: Student ID and current / admit term
Reason and rough plan during the leave
Athletic / military / religious affiliations affecting timing
